title: cymbopogon schoenanthus subsp. laniger
intro: Cymbopogon schoenanthus subsp. laniger, also known as the woolly lemongrass or woolly grass, is a subspecies of the Cymbopogon schoenanthus plant, a type of grass native to Africa and the Middle East. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cymbopogon schoenanthus subsp. laniger include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, teas, and infusions that can be used to reap its medicinal benefits.
